anti_nih_findings.notes: Tagged four-symbol repair overlaps with enriched alphabets and erasure-style side information, but this stage keeps semantic MAP bits unchanged and uses tags only as sparse recovery evidence.
The causal idea resembles decoder-informed selective redundancy, but no existing repo primitive already performs conflict-localized sparse repair on open-world factor domains.

anti_nih_findings.family: sparse codes / sparse block codes / spreading codes
anti_nih_findings.notes: These are the honest redundancy controls; Arm F and random-tag controls are included so the stage cannot claim victory from generic extra bits.
Marker overlays act like sparse confidence hints, not canonical identity or pointer payloads.
Reuse the native semantic decoder and only add a thin marker-aware cleanup wrapper, conflict attribution, and patch protocol.

why_not_scratch: A new resonator, new VSA algebra, or learned encoder would answer a different question than the narrow causal repair hypothesis.
The repository already contains deterministic MAP domain generation, resonator iteration budgets, and exact reconstruction checks.
This stage only needs a versioned recovery view, conflict accounting, sparse tag placement, and matched controls.
